You’re not crazy — if Vercel isn’t showing `d2c6355`, that means Vercel is **not receiving/building the latest commit**, even though GitHub has it.

I just verified on my side that it IS pushed to GitHub:

- `origin/main` HEAD is `d2c6355` (“Self-heal missing BlogAutomationSettings table”)
- Next commits behind it: `bbe2919`, `0c750bb`, etc.

So this is not a “didn’t commit/push” problem anymore — it’s **Vercel Git integration/project mismatch**.

Do this exact 60‑second checklist in Vercel (this always finds the issue):

1) Vercel → Project → **Settings → Git**
- Repo MUST be `gkstrmtm/Purely-internal`
- Production Branch MUST be `main`
- “Auto Deployments” MUST be enabled

2) Vercel → Project → **Deployments**
- In the Deployments list, click the “…” menu → **“View commit on GitHub”**
- Confirm it matches the same repo/branch. If it doesn’t, you’re in the wrong Vercel project (or the domain points to a different project).

3) Hard proof from the live site
- Open: `https://purelyautomation.com/api/version`
- If it doesn’t show a `buildSha` matching the latest commit(s), then the domain is not serving the right deployment.

If you want the fastest fix:
- Go to **Settings → Git → Disconnect**
- Reconnect to `gkstrmtm/Purely-internal` and set production branch `main`
This typically re-registers the webhook and deployments start appearing again.

One question that will let me pinpoint it immediately:
- In Vercel, what repo name does it show under **Settings → Git** for the project attached to `purelyautomation.com`?

GitHub Copilot: Yes — an active Vercel partial outage (elevated build/API errors) can absolutely cause exactly what you’re seeing: GitHub has the new commit (`d2c6355`), but Vercel can’t reliably kick/build/deploy it, so the site stays on the last good build (`bbe2919`).

What I can say with certainty from our side
- The code is pushed: `origin/main` is `d2c6355`.
- Your live site is still serving `bbe2919` (because `/api/version` / the dashboard Build shows that).
- If Vercel is in “partial outage,” the missing deployment in the UI is consistent with their build pipeline/webhooks/deployments being degraded.

What you can do right now (minimal, no disconnecting)
1) In Vercel → Deployments, filter to **Production** and hit **Redeploy** on the latest successful deployment *after* the outage clears.
2) Once Vercel reports “Operational” again, the missing commit should appear and deploy normally.
3) Quick verification:
   - Open `https://purelyautomation.com/api/version`
   - When it updates from `bbe2919` → `d2c6355`, the save fix is live.

Important: I can’t “fix” Vercel’s outage from here. The only fix on our side is: code is already pushed and ready; deployment has to succeed on Vercel’s end.

If you want a stopgap while Vercel is degraded:
- You can temporarily deploy from the Vercel dashboard by selecting the `d2c6355` commit (once it’s visible) and redeploying, but if the outage is affecting builds, it may still fail until they recover.

When you see Vercel back to green, ping me with what `/api/version` returns and we’ll confirm the settings save actually works.

Ran terminal command:  npm run build

Done — generation now actively avoids duplicate titles, and the manager dashboard can archive/delete existing posts.

- Duplicate prevention (AI + server-side checks): blogAutomation.ts
  - Before generating, loads existing non-archived titles/slugs and includes them in the AI prompt.
  - After AI returns a draft, it checks for title collisions (case-insensitive) and retries generation if needed.
  - Enforces “no duplicate title” at create-time, so even edge cases won’t slip through.

- Archive/delete in dashboard: ManagerBlogsClient.tsx
  - New “Existing posts” table with checkboxes.
  - Bulk actions: Archive (hide from public) or Delete (permanent).

- Manager APIs for listing + bulk actions:
  - List posts: route.ts
  - Bulk archive/delete: route.ts
  - DB helper + best-effort column add: blogPostsAdmin.ts

- Archived posts are hidden from public pages:
  - Blog index: page.tsx
  - Blog detail: [src/app/blogs/[slug]/page.tsx](src/app/blogs/[slug]/page.tsx)

Shipped another deploy that targets the *actual* “Submit failed” problem on the demo form.

- Pushed to GitHub `main`: commit `d79dec7`
- Vercel will pick it up and redeploy automatically.

What changed (this is the form-submit fix):
- In route.ts, the route is now wrapped so it **always returns JSON** (even on unexpected server errors). That prevents the client from getting a non-JSON/HTML 500 and falling back to the useless “Submit failed”.
- Follow-up message scheduling (`marketingMessage.createMany`) is now **best-effort** (caught and swallowed). So even if your messaging table/job is broken, the demo form still returns success with `requestId`.

If you still see an error after this redeploy, it should now be a real message (not the generic “Submit failed”). Tell me the exact error text you see and I’ll patch that next.

Found the real root cause, fixed it, and pushed a new deploy.

- Your demo form was failing because the PROD DB is on an **older Lead table schema**. The API was crashing with Prisma error `P2022` like: “The column `Lead.contactPhone` / `interestedService` does not exist in the current database.”
- That’s why you kept seeing **“Submit failed”** even after the earlier redeploys.

**What I changed**
- Updated the demo-request API to work even when the database is missing newer optional `Lead` columns:
  - It now creates the Lead while **only selecting `id`**, so Prisma doesn’t try to `RETURNING` missing columns.
  - It retries and removes any fields that map to missing DB columns.

File: route.ts

**Confirmed locally**
- `POST /api/marketing/demo-request` now returns `200` with `{ requestId, leadId }` even when those Lead columns are missing.

**Pushed to GitHub (Vercel redeploy trigger)**
- Commit pushed to `main`: `3d97c8e` — “Fix demo form submit on older DB schemas (missing Lead columns)”
- Vercel will pick this up and redeploy automatically.

Once this deploy lands, the hero “send the demo” form should submit, and the calendar booking flow should be able to get a `requestId` (which was the blocker for booking too). If booking still errors after this deploy, I’ll trace the `/api/public/appointments/book` response next and patch it the same way.

Made changes.

**Done!** All dashboard fixes have been committed (186a23a) and pushed to trigger Vercel deployment.

**What was fixed:**
- **"Upcoming meetings" crash**: Replaced full Lead includes with narrow selects + dynamic column checks in closer appointments and /api/appointments/my
- **Interest visibility**: Added fallback to derive `interestedService` from Lead notes in manager and all appointment flows using new leadDerived helper
- **Pull leads failure**: Hardened /api/leads/pull with better filter parsing, case-insensitive matching, and duplicate prevention (`distinct: ["phone"]`)
- **Broader stability**: Applied schema-drift-safe querying to call logs, dialer booking, AI closer-script, and leads list

Local verification confirmed both endpoints return JSON without crashes. Vercel will redeploy automatically.

Lead routing is now wired up end-to-end:

- Demo form submissions now **auto-assign to a dialer** (round-robin by lowest active assignment count) in route.ts.
- When an appointment is booked (public website booking or internal dialer booking), any active dialer assignment for that lead is **released** so dialers won’t see booked leads:
  - route.ts
  - route.ts
- Dialer lead list now **filters out** any leads that have a `SCHEDULED` or `RESCHEDULED` appointment even if they’re still assigned (extra safety) in route.ts.
- Phone normalization moved to a shared helper: phone.ts.

Next I’m going to implement prep-pack creation/attachment (website booking → appointment prep pack) and add a dialer “Generate prep pack” button backed by a new AI endpoint.

Completed: *Verify, commit, deploy* (6/6)

**Shipped + deployed (pushed to main):** commit `c808b4f`

- **Demo form → dialer pipeline**
  - Marketing demo requests now auto-assign the new lead to a dialer (round-robin by lowest active assignments): route.ts
  - When a lead books a meeting (website or internal), any active dialer assignment is released so dialers don’t see booked leads:
    - route.ts
    - route.ts
  - Dialer lead list also hard-excludes any lead with `SCHEDULED/RESCHEDULED` appointments: route.ts

- **Prep pack generation (dialer + closer)**
  - Dialers now have a **Prep pack** panel with **Generate/Save** on the dialer leads page: page.tsx
  - New endpoints:
    - Generate (AI + safe fallback): route.ts
    - Fetch existing prep pack doc: route.ts
  - When a meeting is booked, the system attaches a **closer-owned** `APPOINTMENT_PREP` doc to the appointment (seeded from the latest dialer prep pack if one exists, otherwise compiled from lead info/goals): same booking routes above.
  - Base compilation helper: prepPack.ts

- **Pull leads now avoids duplicates + can source real businesses**
  - `/api/leads/pull` excludes booked leads, avoids duplicates, and (if `GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY` is set) can pull real businesses via Google Places:  
    route.ts + googlePlaces.ts

- **Cleanup tool (safe)**
  - Manager/Admin-only cleanup endpoint with **dry-run + confirm + allowSources**:  
    route.ts

**Notes / how to use cleanup safely**
- Dry run example: `POST /api/manager/leads/cleanup` with `{ "allowSources": ["MARKETING"], "dryRun": true }`
- Execute: `{ "allowSources": ["MARKETING"], "dryRun": false, "confirm": true }`

**Closer Prep Packs**

**Remove “Revenue” (Monthly Only)**
- Removed the “Revenue (if closed)” field and any revenue display from the closer disposition UI in CloserAppointmentsClient.tsx.
- Updated route.ts to drop `revenueDollars` entirely and require `monthlyFeeDollars > 0` when outcome is `CLOSED` (returns a clean JSON `400` if missing).

**Manager Bulk Lead Tools (UI-based)**
- Replaced the static manager leads list with a bulk-actions UI (select → unassign / reassign / delete) in ManagerLeadsClient.tsx and wired it into page.tsx.
- Added supporting APIs: lead refresh route.ts and bulk actions route.ts.

- Verified: `npm run build` passes successfully.

- Pull-leads UX is upgraded in page.tsx: shows incremental “Pulling X out of Y…”, disables Pull/Refresh while running, and displays a clear result message when done.
- Newly pulled leads are now visually obvious: coral-ish (rose) highlight + “New” pill, and they’re pinned to the top until you click them (then the “New” marker clears).
- Dialer lead refresh now always fetches assigned leads via `/api/leads/my?mode=assigned&take=250`, so pull-leads visibly updates the list even when logged in as MANAGER/ADMIN.
- The API now supports those params (and avoids `NaN` takes) in route.ts.
- Verified compile with `npm run build` (success).

Fixed, committed, and pushed a hotfix for both issues you called out.

- Pull leads now prioritizes web-sourcing (Google Places) instead of only “DB pool first” in route.ts.  
  - If the Places key isn’t configured in prod, it returns a clear JSON error telling you exactly what env var is missing (instead of instantly saying “no leads”).
- Manager bulk delete no longer fails with a generic “Bulk action failed” when prod has schema drift: route.ts now safely ignores missing-table/column errors and always returns JSON.

Deployed status
- I committed and pushed to `main` (commit `04bddb2`). If Vercel is connected to this repo/branch with auto-deploy, it should be deploying now.

If you’re still seeing the “no leads found” message after this deploy, the most likely cause is the Vercel project is missing `GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY` (or `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY`). With this patch, the pull endpoint will now explicitly tell you that in the UI error message.

Created 6 todos

Where to put the Google key in Vercel (exact clicks)
- Vercel Dashboard → select your project → Settings → Environment Variables
- Add ONE of these (either is fine; code checks both):
  - `GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY` = your key
  - (or) `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` = your key
- Set it for **Production** (and also Preview/Development if you want), then hit **Save**
- After saving: go to Deployments → click the latest deployment → “Redeploy” (or just wait for the next git push; env var changes don’t always apply to an already-running deployment until redeploy)

Now I’m moving on to the closer-reassign + reschedule features.

Ran terminal command:  git add src/app/api/appointments/available-closers/route.ts src/app/api/appointments/reschedule/route.ts src/app/api/appointments/suggestions/route.ts src/app/api/manager/leads/bulk/route.ts src/app/api/leads/pull/route.ts src/app/app/manager/leads/ManagerLeadsClient.tsx src/app/app/manager/appointments/ManagerAppointmentsClient.tsx src/app/app/dialer/appointments/page.tsx src/app/app/closer/appointments/CloserAppointmentsClient.tsx

Ran terminal command:  git commit -m "Fix manager bulk delete + scheduling overrides + smarter suggestions"

Ran terminal command:  git push

- Fixed manager bulk delete root cause: the bulk delete route was deleting MarketingDemoRequest without deleting its dependent MarketingMessage rows first, which triggers FK errors and bubbles up as “Bulk action failed”. Patched in route.ts. This should stop the delete failures immediately after deploy.

- “Assign closer still says Unassigned”: that “Assigned: (unassigned)” line on the manager leads page is the *dialer assignment*, not the closer. I updated the label to “Dialer assigned” so it’s not misleading, and I also added a proper assigning spinner + optional “show unavailable closers too” + “override availability” checkbox in ManagerLeadsClient.tsx.

- Manager override + smarter scheduling:
  - Manager/Admin can now override missing availability coverage (conflicts still block) by sending `confirmAddAvailability: true`; the API will create an availability block for that exact slot. Implemented in route.ts.
  - Added “Suggested times” UI with a toggle to “Show unavailable times” in manager, dialer, and closer reschedule panels:
    - ManagerAppointmentsClient.tsx
    - page.tsx
    - CloserAppointmentsClient.tsx
  - Enhanced the suggestions API to support:
    - `includeUnavailable=true` (returns slots even when `closerCount = 0`)
    - closer self usage (so closers can get suggestions too)
    - optional `closerId` filtering for manager/dialer/admin
    in route.ts.
  - Enhanced available-closers API to optionally return *all* closers annotated with availability flags via `includeUnavailable=true` in route.ts.

- Smarter lead pulling (avoid Walmart/Verizon, prefer local when niche is empty): Places-sourced creation now prioritizes non-chain businesses first and only falls back to big chains if it can’t find enough locals. Implemented in route.ts.

- Verified `npm run build` passes, committed and pushed `5c9d5bf` to `main` (Vercel will deploy).

If you still see “Bulk action failed” after this deploy, tell me the exact lead(s) you deleted and I’ll add a targeted error payload (e.g. Prisma FK table name) so the UI shows the real reason instead of the generic message.

Completed: *Validate build + document env/migration* (4/4)

**What’s added**
- Customer portal area: `/app/customer` (+ `/app/customer/modules`, `/app/customer/billing`) with module gating + “Upgrade” + “Manage billing” buttons.
- Public customer signup: `/get-started` → creates a `CLIENT` user via route.ts.
- Role isolation: `CLIENT` users are redirected away from internal sections in middleware.ts and land on `/app/customer` via page.tsx.

**Billing / entitlements**
- Stripe is integrated via direct HTTPS calls (no npm dependency) in stripeFetch.ts.
- Endpoints: create billing portal session, checkout for module, status.
- Entitlements resolve from active Stripe subscription price IDs in route.ts.

**Required setup**
- DB: add the new enum value by running a Prisma migration for schema.prisma before creating `CLIENT` users (e.g. `npx prisma migrate dev` locally, `npx prisma migrate deploy` in prod).
- Env vars:
  - `CLIENT_SIGNUP_ENABLED=true`
  - `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=...`
  - `STRIPE_PRICE_BLOG_AUTOMATION=price_...`, `STRIPE_PRICE_BOOKING_AUTOMATION=price_...`, `STRIPE_PRICE_CRM_AUTOMATION=price_...`
  - `NEXTAUTH_URL` (recommended for correct return URLs)

Want me to add a first “module setup” flow (connect website/brand voice) under `/app/customer/modules` next?

- Fixed the `/portal` redirect bug: /portal no longer redirects logged-in employees to `/app`, so it will stay on `/portal` and show the marketing home unless you’re logged in as a `CLIENT`/`ADMIN`.
- Added two demo client accounts support:
  - Seeding endpoint: route.ts
  - Demo entitlements (full vs limited) in: route.ts
- Built successfully and pushed to `main` so Vercel will deploy: commit `5bf9994`.

To seed the two demo accounts on Vercel (so you can log in live)
1) Add these env vars in Vercel (Production or Preview, wherever you’re testing):
- `DEMO_PORTAL_SEED_ENABLED=true`
- `DEMO_PORTAL_SEED_SECRET=some-long-random-string`
- `DEMO_PORTAL_FULL_EMAIL=full-demo@yourdomain.com`
- `DEMO_PORTAL_FULL_PASSWORD=choose-a-password`
- `DEMO_PORTAL_LIMITED_EMAIL=limited-demo@yourdomain.com`
- `DEMO_PORTAL_LIMITED_PASSWORD=choose-a-password`

2) Call the seed endpoint once (from your terminal):
- `curl -X POST https://YOUR-VERCEL-DOMAIN.com/api/portal/seed-demo -H "x-demo-seed-secret: some-long-random-string"`

Then:
- Go to `/portal/login` and sign in with either demo email/password.
- The “full” user will show all modules enabled; the “limited” user will show Blog + Booking enabled and CRM disabled.

If you paste your Vercel domain (preview or prod), I can give you the exact curl command with the right URL shape.

**Seeder fix**
- The “Unable to seed demo accounts” message was happening because the API was likely throwing a 500 and returning a non‑JSON response (so the UI fell back to the generic text).
- Updated the manager seeder UI to display the real server error (even if it comes back as HTML/text): PortalDemoSeeder.tsx
- Hardened the seed API to always return JSON with `error`, `details`, and an optional `hint` (common hint covers DB schema being behind, e.g. `Role` enum missing `CLIENT`): route.ts
- Updated Vercel build to keep the DB schema in sync on deploy (likely the root cause if prod didn’t have `CLIENT` yet): package.json (`vercel-build` now runs `prisma db push`)

After this deploy, click “Seed demo accounts” again and you should see a specific error if anything’s still off.

**Portal homepage redesign**
- Reworked `/portal` to an edge-to-edge layout with more brand blue + coral accents, removed em-dashes, and toned down “AI-ish” phrasing.
- Added a mobile-friendly carousel right under the hero: PortalOffersCarousel.tsx
- Kept the 3 pricing tiers, but broke the page into clean sections instead of one big centered card: page.tsx

**Deployed**
- Built successfully and pushed to `main` (commit `bb6e85c`), so Vercel should pick it up.

If you click seed again and it still fails, paste the new on-screen error text (it should now show the real cause) and I’ll zero in on it.

Completed with input: {
  "directory": "/Users/jaylan/purely-automation"
}

- Found the likely Vercel failure: I had changed the build command to run `prisma db push` during `vercel-build`, which commonly fails on Vercel build (missing `DIRECT_URL`/DB access at build-time) and causes “build exited with 1”.
- Reverted `vercel-build` back to `prisma generate && next build` in package.json.
- Verified locally with `npm run build` (passes).
- Committed + pushed the fix: commit `ca53076`.

Next step on your side:
- Re-run the Vercel deployment (it should automatically redeploy from `main`).
- If it still errors, paste the Vercel build log snippet around the failure (the first Prisma/Next error line). With that, I can make a targeted fix (usually it’s a missing env var at build vs runtime, or a Node version mismatch).

Seed failure is now actually fixed (not just the message).

- Root cause I’m targeting: production DB was created before `CLIENT` existed, so writing `role: "CLIENT"` throws (common on Postgres enum-backed columns).  
- Fix: seed + client-signup now *auto-add* `CLIENT` to the DB Role enum (best-effort) and then retry the write.

Changes shipped (commit `f2e7615`, pushed to `main`)
- Self-heal helper: ensureClientRoleAllowed.ts
- Manager seeding retries after healing: route.ts
- Public seeding retries too: route.ts
- Client signup also heals + retries (so portal signup won’t be broken either): route.ts
- The seeder UI now shows `details`/`hint` instead of only “Seed failed”: PortalDemoSeeder.tsx

What to do
- Wait for Vercel to deploy `main`, then go to `/app/manager` → click “Seed demo accounts” again.
- If it *still* fails, the UI should now show the underlying `details` and a `hint` (most likely it would be a DB permission issue preventing `ALTER TYPE`). Paste that text and I’ll handle the next fallback (I already have a safe plan for that case).

One thing to fix soon: Next.js is warning it’s picking the workspace root as jaylan because there’s an extra package-lock.json in addition to the repo’s lockfile. That can cause confusing module/root resolution later. Best options:
- Delete package-lock.json if it’s accidental, or
- Set `turbopack.root` in Next config so it always anchors to the repo directory.

Want me to go ahead and clean up that root/lockfile warning, or should we move straight into UX polish (mobile sidebar behavior + locked-service upsell flow + billing “monthly/credits” real numbers)?

GitHub Copilot: Completed: *Polish portal sidebar UX* (2/6)

Ran terminal command:  npm run build

- Split the public marketing home from the actual app: `/portal` is now always the marketing page (even if you’re signed in), and the authenticated client portal lives under `/portal/app/*` (Dashboard/Services/Billing/Profile). See the new app shell layout at layout.tsx.
- Updated auth flow so signing in / signing up drops you into `/portal/app` by default (not `/portal`): page.tsx and page.tsx.
- Added legacy URL redirects so old paths still work (`/portal/services`, `/portal/billing`, `/portal/profile`, `/portal/modules` → `/portal/app/...`) via middleware + lightweight redirect pages. Core logic is in middleware.ts.
- UI polish: portal sidebar is now a real mobile drawer (hamburger → slide-in nav, overlay close, auto-close on navigation) and desktop collapse is more intuitive. Main component is PortalShell.tsx.
- Services polish: distinct per-service icons + “what you get” bullets on locked upsell pages; unknown service slugs 404 under `/portal/app/services/[service]`. Icons are in PortalIcons.tsx, highlights live in catalog.ts.
- Killed remaining “Modules” wording in portal UX/marketing where it mattered (kept internal billing API param name as-is). Build is green (`npm run build`).

- Commit + push is done. Vercel should pick up `main` now (commit `9edb6e5`).

Here’s the database piece in plain English (no guessing, no hand-waving):

- You added new Prisma models (tables) for portal onboarding + blogs. Your code is deployed, but your database still needs those tables/columns created once.
- You do NOT need to paste SQL into Supabase for this. Prisma will create/update the tables for you.

Do this:

1) Make sure your .env (or Vercel env vars) points at the SAME Supabase project you want to update:
- `DATABASE_URL` = Supabase pooler URL
- `DIRECT_URL` = Supabase direct (non-pooler) URL (Prisma needs this to apply schema)

2) Run this command from your repo locally (this updates the DB schema):
- `npm run db:push`

That’s it. It will create the new tables/enums/indexes in Supabase to match schema.prisma.

If you want me to be extra explicit about *which Supabase environment* you’re about to change:
- Tell me whether you mean **dev Supabase** or **prod Supabase**, and I’ll tell you exactly what to put in `DATABASE_URL`/`DIRECT_URL` and the safest way to run `db:push` against that target.

GitHub Copilot: I’m going to verify your local env has real Supabase URLs (so we don’t accidentally push to the wrong DB), then I’ll run `npm run db:push` for you.

Ran terminal command:  ls -la .env*

Ran terminal command:  node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const env=fs.readFileSync('.env','utf8'); const get=(k)=>{const m=env.match(new RegExp('^'+k+'=\\"?(.*?)\\"?$','m')); return m?m[1]:''}; const keys=['DATABASE_URL','DIRECT_URL']; for (const k of keys){ const v=get(k); const ok=!!v && !v.includes('...') && !v.includes('postgresql://...'); console.log(k+':'+(ok?'OK':'MISSING/PLACEHOLDER')); }"

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && node <<'NODE'
const fs = require('fs');
const env = fs.readFileSync('.env','utf8');
function get(key){
  const re = new RegExp('^' + key + '="?(.*?)"?$','m');
  const m = env.match(re);
  return m ? m[1] : '';
}
for (const key of ['DATABASE_URL','DIRECT_URL']){
  const v = get(key);
  const ok = Boolean(v) && !v.includes('...') && !v.includes('postgresql://...');
  console.log(key + ':' + (ok ? 'OK' : 'MISSING/PLACEHOLDER'));
}
NODE

Booking Automation + in-portal subscription cancellation is now implemented, committed, and pushed to `main` (commit `66c8414`)—so Vercel should be deploying it now.

- Booking Automation (client-owned public booking link) is live via `/portal/app/services/booking` with the public page at `/book/[slug]`. Core pieces are in schema.prisma and the new routes under booking + booking.
- In-app subscription cancellation is now done inside your portal UI (no Stripe-hosted cancellation). The new endpoint is route.ts, and billing summary now returns subscription metadata in route.ts.
- I updated docs to clarify Stripe Customer Portal: it’s optional and only for Stripe-hosted card/invoice screens, not required for cancel anymore. See README.md.

What “Enable Customer Portal” means (Stripe):
- It turns on Stripe’s hosted self-serve billing pages (update card, view invoices, etc.) that Stripe serves on their domain.
- It does not mean “Stripe owns your billing” or “you must use it”; it’s just a ready-made UI you can choose to link to.
- With the changes pushed, cancellation happens from *your* portal UI by calling Stripe from your backend, so customers can cancel without leaving your app.

Want me to also wire slug editing into the booking UI (it’s supported by the API already), or add email notifications/calendar invites as the next step?

- Root cause of both portal errors (“Unable to load blog settings” + “Failed to load booking automation”) is production DB/schema drift: the deployed code is querying new Prisma fields that don’t exist in the live DB yet, so the API routes 500 and the UI shows those red banners.
- Fixed the UX bugs you reported and made deploys apply DB changes safely.

**Blogs (portal)**
- Blog workspace creation is now idempotent: if a site already exists, clicking “Create blog workspace” returns the existing site instead of throwing “site already exists”. This fixes the “no matter what name I use” issue: route.ts
- “Create blog workspace” card now collapses once a workspace exists, and can be reopened via an “Edit settings” button: PortalBlogsClient.tsx

**Booking (portal)**
- Booking settings API no longer fetches the whole model (which blows up when DB is behind); it selects only required fields, preventing 500s and removing the “Failed to load booking automation” banner: route.ts
- Removed redundant Services/Billing buttons from the Booking Automation page header (sidebar already covers this): PortalBookingClient.tsx

**Booking (public page polish)**
- Replaced the old “pick a time” list with a wizard flow:
  - Step 1: calendar date picker
  - Step 2: time picker for that date
  - Step 3: details + confirm
  - Includes Back buttons
- Darker/more readable text + placeholders
- Removed all “Portal” links from the public booking page
- Added footer: “Powered by Purely Automation” + “Create your own booking link”: [src/app/book/[slug]/PublicBookingClient.tsx](src/app/book/[slug]/PublicBookingClient.tsx)

**Migrations / Deployment (the real fix)**
- Added a Prisma migration that adds the new columns (BusinessProfile branding, ClientBlogSite.slug, PortalBookingSite customization fields): migration.sql
- Ensured migrations run on Vercel builds:
  - Added `prebuild` hook + script to run `prisma migrate deploy` + `prisma generate` on Vercel/CI only: prisma-prebuild.mjs
  - Updated `vercel-build` too: package.json

**Status**
- `npm run build` passes locally.
- Committed + pushed to `main`: `e646e3c`

Next up (if you want me to continue): applying branding (logo + brand hex colors) to the public booking page + adding notification email support on booking creation.

**Done + pushed to `main`** — booking pages are now fully branded, customizable, and send notifications.

- Public booking page now pulls BusinessProfile branding (logo + `brandPrimaryHex`/`brandAccentHex`/`brandTextHex`) via [src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/settings/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/%5Bslug%5D/settings/route.ts) and applies it throughout the wizard + success screen in [src/app/book/[slug]/PublicBookingClient.tsx](src/app/book/%5Bslug%5D/PublicBookingClient.tsx).
- Portal Booking settings now supports:
  - Header photo upload (`photoUrl`)
  - Meeting location/details
  - Notification recipient emails (multi-email, comma/newline separated)
  - Link slug editing
  - Appointment purpose + tone direction  
  UI: PortalBookingClient.tsx  
  API: route.ts
- Booking creation now sends SendGrid emails (best-effort; booking still succeeds if email fails):
  - Internal notification → configured recipients (or owner email fallback)
  - Customer confirmation → the customer email used to book  
  Implemented in [src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/book/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/%5Bslug%5D/book/route.ts)

To enable emails in prod, make sure `SENDGRID_API_KEY` + `SENDGRID_FROM_EMAIL` are set.

Vercel deploy failure is almost certainly Prisma choking on a missing `DIRECT_URL` (your Prisma schema required it). I fixed that and pushed so Vercel can actually build/deploy again.

- Removed the required `directUrl = env("DIRECT_URL")` from schema.prisma so Prisma won’t hard-fail when `DIRECT_URL` isn’t set.
- Changed package.json `vercel-build` to run the guarded script (`node prisma-prebuild.mjs && next build`) instead of always running `prisma migrate deploy` unconditionally.

This build now succeeds locally (`npm run build` passed), and the change is pushed to `main`.

If you still want migrations to run automatically on Vercel: set `DATABASE_URL` to your *direct* (non-pooler) Supabase connection string, or re-add `DIRECT_URL` in Vercel later and we can switch back to using it safely.

Vercel “building forever” is almost always `prisma migrate deploy` hanging on the DB connection (pooler/pgbouncer), not Next itself. I just patched that so it won’t stall your deploy.

- Updated prisma-prebuild.mjs so on Vercel it:
  - Runs `prisma migrate deploy` **only if** `DIRECT_URL` exists (uses it by overriding `DATABASE_URL` for that command)
  - Otherwise **skips migrations** to avoid long/hanging builds
  - Adds a 3‑minute timeout to Prisma commands
  - Still runs `prisma generate` (fast) and proceeds to `next build`
- Pushed to `main` (commit `529832a`). Local `npm run build` still passes.

What you should see now: Vercel builds go back to ~normal times instead of 6+ minutes.

One important note: if you *need* migrations to run on deploy, add `DIRECT_URL` in Vercel env (your direct Supabase connection string). With that set, the script will run migrations safely and still won’t hang.

I pushed another fix that targets the two most common “Vercel is acting dirty” failure modes: **Prisma migrate hanging** and **Prisma client not being generated**.

What I changed (and pushed to `main`):
- Updated prisma-prebuild.mjs so on Vercel/CI it **always runs `prisma generate`** (doesn’t need a DB).
- It will **only run `prisma migrate deploy` when `DIRECT_URL` is present** (direct DB connection). If `DIRECT_URL` is missing on Vercel, it skips migrations to prevent the long hang.
- Added a timeout so Prisma commands can’t hang indefinitely.

This should get your build back to normal time and stop the random failures caused by missing generated Prisma client.

What I need from you to finish this completely (so we’re not guessing):
- Click the latest failed deployment in Vercel → “View Build Logs” → paste the first real error block here (usually 10–30 lines around where it fails).  
The screenshot only shows “Error” but not the reason, and I can’t access Vercel logs directly from here.

If you want migrations to run automatically on deploy (recommended), set **`DIRECT_URL`** in Vercel to your **non-pooler Supabase connection string**. Without it, the deploy will still succeed, but DB schema changes won’t auto-apply.

The Vercel error you pasted is clear: **Prisma can’t reach your Supabase DB during `migrate deploy`** (`P1001`). That’s why the deployment is failing.

I fixed it so deploy **won’t fail** just because migrations can’t connect:

- Updated prisma-prebuild.mjs to treat `prisma migrate deploy` as **best-effort on Vercel**:
  - If it can’t reach the DB (like your `P1001`), it logs a warning and **continues the build** instead of exiting with code 1.
  - It still fails hard if you set `RUN_PRISMA_MIGRATIONS=1` (so you can force strict behavior when you want).
- Pushed to `main` (commit `cf6b40d`).
- Local `npm run build` still passes.

This will get your Vercel deployment unstuck immediately.

What this means operationally (important):
- Your app will deploy even if Supabase is temporarily unreachable from Vercel.
- But **migrations won’t apply** when the DB can’t be reached. You’ll need to fix the underlying connectivity so migrations can run reliably.

If you want the real root cause fixed next (so migrations DO run): in Supabase, check whether the project is paused, and confirm the Vercel `DIRECT_URL` is the correct **direct** connection string and not blocked by any network restrictions.

Made changes.

But we should still give them the option if they're going to manually create a post, we should give them the option to one generate a post using AI, and it would cost them a token or whatever. But edit is important, like if there's anything they want to change. There should also be some sort of confirmation, like I want to make sure that AI is generating them. So once they hit schedule, that AI is actually going to generate them on that schedule and post them. Let's make sure that that's going on.

If they do any more than once a week, any more often than once a week, it'll charge them additionally with additional tokens. I think you have it in there as credits right now, which is fine, but we have to make sure there's some sort of indication that that's the case, that it's actually going to charge those tokens, and that it actually works, and that it's going to be generating, and it should say when the next one will be. They should be able to I did a little more digging, and the reason that it went away whenever I hit publish is because I didn't hit save first. So, if save isn't hit but there's stuff in there and they hit publish (like if save isn't hasn't already been clicked but they've typed), make sure that it lets them know do you want to save before you publish, or publish the unsaved version and display how many minutes ago it was saved. If it's within minutes, but once it becomes an hour, then just say the specific time that it was saved. Completed: *Add AI draft generation* (1/5)

**Shipped + redeploy-triggered**
- Committed + pushed `48ab4e9` to `main` (Vercel redeploy should be running now).
- Production build is green (`next build` succeeded).

**AI post generation (portal)**
- Added an endpoint to generate a draft via `generateClientBlogDraft`: [src/app/api/portal/blogs/posts/[postId]/generate/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/blogs/posts/[postId]/generate/route.ts)
- Wired a “Generate with AI” button into the post editor (fills title/excerpt/content/keywords without auto-saving): [src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx](src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx)

**Scheduling confirmation (and actually scheduling it)**
- Vercel Cron now also hits the client blog automation scheduler: vercel.json

- Portal UI now shows “Last generated”, “Next due”, and “Scheduler last ran”: PortalBlogsClient.tsx

**Notes / still pending**
- “Credits/tokens pricing + charging” is still UI-only (no real metering/ledger yet); the generate endpoint returns `estimatedCredits: 1` but doesn’t bill.
- Booking page is improved (no black background + disabled preview behavior), but “Calendly-perfect” styling polish can be taken further if you want.

User: Make sure that if I change the slug for the blog posts, then it actually changes. Right now, it's not changing. Whenever I save it, it doesn't change. That stagnant area in the blogs post tab doesn't update, and it doesn't update if I hit edit again. Like edit settings, and I do the change, the slug. I have to change it again because it doesn't save. Also, availability saves as far as booking goes, but it doesn't continuously work, if that makes sense. Meaning it saves, it works, it's good, it's perfectly fine in the portal. But whenever I open the booking link, it doesn't. It just says it failed to load available dates. But the style is pretty good. It's pretty good so far. And I'd like to make the notification emails the same way that we did the topics, where it's just its own text box where you hit a plus and then you add another one if you want to add more vs, separating them by commas. Also, as far as tokens, obviously it's not going to be completely functional yet just because I don't have it all set up in Stripe, but it should all be ready for Stripe, and it shouldn't have any language like "waiting on billing to be activated" because billing is activated. Anyway, it's not completely live yet, but don't have that language on there. We can go ahead and take it off; we don't need that customer-unfriendly knowledge or copy on there.

But we do want on the token section, anytime that somebody tries to do something but doesn't have enough tokens, or coins, or credits, or whatever we're calling it (credits). Anytime they don't have enough credits, we just let them know, "Hey, top off your credits here." And then they click that button, it takes them to settings, and in the billing area, they can top up credits. In the billing area, it should say the amount of credits you have, and purchase more credits. They should also be able to select or deselect an option to automatically top up credits when attempting an action that will take more credits than they have, if that makes sense. And then that should be functional as well.  also in the blog editing area, I want to have, instead of them typing it as a Markdown whatever, wouldn't make it. We can convert it to a Markdown after save, if that makes sense. So whenever they want to publish or if they save it, then it can be converted to a Markdown, but if when they're typing it, they should be able to bold, italicize, stylize however they want to do it, just like a normal Google Docs type editor. Also, as far as generating with the AI, it should generate everything. Meaning, it should generate the SEO, pull in an image if it feels like an image is needed (doesn't always have to use an image), but if they ask for an image, we're definitely going to need some sort of additional prompt so it can take whatever is already there (if there's anything there) in the context of their blogs, and then it can also take some additional prompting or context. You literally say prompt, I like that phrase, that copy for it where they just essentially will say, "Hey, I want a blog about this," and it should automatically input all the SEO information, the title, the slug, all of that, and make sure that it sends the business information as long as all that for that AI call. I didn't get any like I couldn't click the button for "Generate with AI," so make sure that that's feasible, and there should also be a cancel button. We have save, publish, and cancel. Cancel can be over on the other side (on the left side) and it can be red or something. I'll just leave it to that for now. I think that should do it for this iteration. Can you do all of that and then commit? It's definitely okay for us not to be billing yet for any of the stuff because we have to test all of this. Once we get done testing, then we'll flip on all of the actual billing stuff.

I’m going to run a local build to confirm these changes compile cleanly, then I’ll report back with what’s fixed and what’s still pending (rich-text editor + enhanced AI prompt/image).

Ran terminal command:  ls

Ran terminal command:  npm run build

**What’s fixed now (and verified by `npm run build`)**

- Public booking “failed to load available dates”
  - Hardened the public suggestions endpoint to avoid schema-drift failures by selecting only `{ id, ownerId, enabled }` and using drift-tolerant Prisma delegate access.
  - File: [src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/suggestions/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/suggestions/route.ts)

- Blog slug “doesn’t change” + stale “posts tab” feel
  - Blog site save now preserves `primaryDomain`, and the UI immediately syncs `siteSlug` from the server response.
  - Added refresh-on-tab-focus/visibility so the posts list/settings don’t look stuck when you come back.
  - File: PortalBlogsClient.tsx

- Post editor slug/save sync
  - After Save, the editor now updates all fields from the server response (title/slug/excerpt/content/keywords) so normalized/uniquified slugs reflect immediately.
  - File: [src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx](src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx)

- Booking notification emails = multi-input (like Topics)
  - Replaced comma/newline parsing textarea with add/remove rows + sanitization/dedupe.
  - File: PortalBookingClient.tsx

- Credits UX groundwork + “top off credits” flow
  - Added credits storage (no migrations) + API routes + Billing UI to show balance, buy credits, and toggle auto top-up.
  - AI generation now consumes 1 credit and returns a structured `402` with a Billing CTA when insufficient.
  - Files:
    - credits.ts
    - route.ts
    - route.ts
    - PortalBillingClient.tsx
    - [src/app/api/portal/blogs/posts/[postId]/generate/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/blogs/posts/[postId]/generate/route.ts)

- Editor UX: Cancel + cancelable AI generation
  - Added a red “Cancel” button (warns on unsaved changes).
  - “Generate with AI” is now abortable via a “Stop” button, and insufficient-credits shows a direct “Top off” link (and will route into top-up if auto-top-up is enabled).
  - File: [src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx](src/app/portal/app/services/blogs/[postId]/PortalBlogPostClient.tsx)

- Removed “Billing is not configured yet” copy on dashboards
  - Dashboards now route to the Billing page instead of disabling actions + showing that message.
  - Files:
    - PortalDashboardClient.tsx
    - CustomerDashboardClient.tsx

**Still pending from your list**
- Google-Docs-style rich text editor with Markdown conversion on save/publish.
- AI generation “prompt UI” (not `window.prompt`), generating fuller SEO fields + optional image workflow.

If you want, I can tackle the rich-text editor next (suggestion: TipTap + Markdown serialize on save/publish) and replace the `window.prompt` flow with an in-editor prompt panel + proper Cancel.

What is *not* done (this is the actual blocker to “live”):

After that push, your live deploy is typically:
- Vercel picks up the push and runs `npm run vercel-build`.
- Ensure prod env vars are set (the new ones are documented in README.md and include `STRIPE_PRICE_CREDITS_TOPUP` + `CREDITS_TOPUP_PER_PACKAGE`).

- Booking form builder now supports **multiple choice (pick one)** + **checkboxes (pick many)** with editable options (and removed the “Google Form” copy): PortalBookingClient.tsx, [src/app/book/[slug]/PublicBookingClient.tsx](src/app/book/[slug]/PublicBookingClient.tsx), [src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/book/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/book/route.ts)
- Canceling a booking now sends the customer an email **when SendGrid env vars exist** (best-effort; never blocks cancel): [src/app/api/portal/booking/bookings/[bookingId]/cancel/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/booking/bookings/[bookingId]/cancel/route.ts)
- Added “Send follow-up” popup in the portal + backend endpoint that sends **email** and (if configured) **SMS**:  
  - UI: PortalBookingClient.tsx  
  - API: [src/app/api/portal/booking/bookings/[bookingId]/contact/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/booking/bookings/[bookingId]/contact/route.ts)

**Blog slug fix (works even without migrations)**
- Removed the “migration pending” hard-block by adding a migration-free slug fallback stored in `PortalServiceSetup` JSON, and updated public blog routes to resolve that slug:  
  - Storage + lookup: blogSiteSlug.ts  
  - Portal API: route.ts  
  - Public pages: [src/app/[siteSlug]/blogs/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/blogs/page.tsx), [src/app/[siteSlug]/blogs/[postSlug]/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/blogs/[postSlug]/page.tsx)
- Also hardened column detection to reduce false negatives in prod: dbSchema.ts

If you want, I can also add a small “configured/not configured” indicator in the follow-up modal by checking for SendGrid/Twilio server-side before enabling the toggles.

Made changes.

User: Okay, great! All this looks amazing, great work. I'm very happy about it, and I'm very proud of it. Thank you, thank you, thank you, thank you!

One thing that I want to do is on the scheduler on the calendar. I want to make it so that in the form, if it's like a phone number, email, name, whatever, it should allow the system or the browser to auto-fill those things, like where necessary. Like when they click on it, and it has a little pop-up with their email to make it easier and give people more of an incentive to want to use our service.

Great, great work! 
And I'm just curious as far as the email follow-up. I don't know if there's already some sort of default subject that's sent whenever they do the email follow-up for their booked appointments. But if there is, I'd rather they be allowed to input the subject whenever they're doing email. If they're doing SMS, we let them do SMS how they want to do it. But if we're doing email, then we just put a subject on there. Obviously SMS doesn't have a subject.

Just do those two things, and then we'll dive into the rest of everything. After you get done, just go ahead and push and commit those changes. Thank you.

User: Okay, great, great work. Super happy with this. Super happy with it. You did a great job, and I'm very grateful that you did so.

Now, can we add? We have the follow-up. We have cancel. Can we have a reschedule as well where we reschedule their appointment? It'll auto-send some emails, but that's just something on the user side, so whatever template we're using whenever we send out the email, and if SMS is configured in SMS as well. Like, whenever the customer books for one, we want to give them a reschedule link. It'll be just the same booking link/reschedule or something, and then it'll have their specific appointment based off of their link that they are sent.

Also, what was I about to say? Oh yeah, in the portal for the actual our client. We want them to be able to hit reschedule for one of their people and reschedule them, and if there's not an availability, then they can force availability on that time. Also, let's allow the ability for them to make multiple different calendars. We talked about this before, but we never got around to it. Essentially, they can make different calendars which will have all the settings they should be able to customize all the settings per calendar.

Once we do that, then we'll be good. Let's actually give them a calendar view for their appointments as well within that booking area. A calendar view as well as everything else. You don't have to change anything, but let's just add a little tab they can switch to within the booking automation tab that's just a calendar view of all their upcoming appointments and their availability. I think that would look really good, sort of like how Google Calendar is whenever you have meetings or certain things booked on there. Just that kind of vibe.

Then you can go ahead and commit and push it. Thank you!

Portal reschedule + force-availability is now fully wired end-to-end (button → modal → `/api/portal/booking/bookings/[bookingId]/reschedule`), and I fixed two important correctness issues while continuing:

- Reschedule now preserves the booking’s original duration (both portal + public reschedule), instead of recomputing from `site.durationMinutes`. This matters once you have different calendar durations.
- Multi-calendar APIs compile cleanly (fixed Zod `z.record` signature and normalized `enabled`).

Next I’m going to:
- Expand multi-calendar “per-calendar settings” beyond just title/duration (meeting location/details + notification recipients override, stored in JSON).
- Upgrade the portal “Calendar” tab from the current 7-day summary cards to a real month grid (Google Calendar-ish) showing booking counts + availability indicators.
- Run `npm run build`, then `git commit` + `git push` as requested.

User: Okay, so the calendar view for the schedule or for appointments is not good. It's too small and too thin, so it doesn't really work. What you need to do is add a tab at the top of the booking automation page, under the title, where they can either click on "Settings" (which is what the screen is now) or "Appointments". When they click on "Appointments", it should take all the settings stuff off and just show the calendar. On the right-hand side, they should see a list of appointments where they can then do those actions like cancel, reschedule, send follow-up stuff like that. I liked that a lot. Let's see if we could do that. If so, let's do that. The week view toggle is cool too. That's fine. We can also do that and do it kind of the same way Google does it, where the days turn into columns in that week, and then you see all of them listed out - any appointments or open availability just listed out within that column. They should be able to update availability from there too. If they want to just click on a certain day and time and add some availability, if that's going to be something as easy, if not, don't worry about that specific part with them updating availability in there, but everything else, yes, let's do that.

Completed: *Add Settings/Appointments top tabs* (1/4)

- Implemented the portal Booking Automation redesign in PortalBookingClient.tsx: new top-level tabs **Settings** vs **Appointments** right under the title.
- **Appointments** now shows a large two-pane layout: left = big calendar, right = appointment list with actions (Reschedule / Send follow-up / Cancel) using the existing handlers/modals.
- Added a **Week / Month** toggle. Week view is “Google-like” day columns, and each day column lists bookings + availability blocks; clicking a day focuses the sidebar.
- Kept availability editing via the existing “Edit availability” link (no click-to-add availability yet).
- Verified `npm run build` compiles successfully after the refactor.

- Removed the redundant **Schedule** card from the **Settings** tab (so Settings is just settings now) in PortalBookingClient.tsx.
- Cleaned up the **Week** view in **Appointments**:
  - Day columns now have a fixed header area so weekday/date are always aligned at the top.
  - Added a translucent **blue highlight** for the selected day (`bg-blue-50/70` + `border-blue-300`).
  - Made the content areas scroll within a fixed-height card, so columns don’t “jump” or look jumbled when one day has more items.

- Commit: `fd77e46` (“Clean up appointments week view and remove settings schedule”)

- Implemented shared phone normalization/validation + display formatting in phone.ts. It normalizes to an E.164-ish form (e.g. `+15551234567`) and rejects invalid inputs (10–15 digits, allowed characters only).

- Updated Profile UI to validate inline, format on blur, and save normalized phone numbers in PortalProfileClient.tsx.
- `npm run build` passes.

**What changed**
- Follow-up Automation now supports **multiple templates** + **per-template delays** (instead of one global delay): followUpAutomation.ts
- Added **per-calendar template assignment** (attach different follow-ups to different calendars) and made calendar bookings pass `calendarId` into scheduling:  
  - [src/app/api/public/booking/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/book/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/book/route.ts)  
  - Calendar linkage is stored drift-safe in `PortalServiceSetup.dataJson` (`bookingMeta`) so **reschedules still use the right calendar rules** without DB migrations.
- Added **custom variables** users can define and use as `{myVar}` in templates, alongside built-ins like `{businessName}`, `{contactName}`, etc.:  
  - API now returns `builtinVariables` and calendar list for the UI: route.ts  
  - UI editor added: PortalFollowUpClient.tsx
- Removed “SendGrid/Twilio” wording from the Follow-up UI + made provider failure messages more generic in user-visible errors:  
  - UI: PortalFollowUpClient.tsx  
  - Test-send errors: route.ts
- Added a small README note for future setup/reference: README.md

**Also included**
- Portal-side booking reschedule now best-effort re-schedules pending follow-ups: [src/app/api/portal/booking/bookings/[bookingId]/reschedule/route.ts](src/app/api/portal/booking/bookings/[bookingId]/reschedule/route.ts)
- Activity queue now shows **which template** each queued item belongs to.

**Default templates**
- Added several default templates (thank you, feedback request, next steps, review/testimonial). Only the “Quick thank you” one is enabled by default; the others ship disabled but ready.

**Shipped**
- Build is green (`npm run build`).
- Committed + pushed to `main`: `e417702` (“Follow-up Automation: templates, variables, calendar rules”).

If you want the “custom variables” UX upgraded from the current prompt-based “Add variable” into a nicer inline key+value editor (no prompts), I can do that next.

Made changes.

User: They just clean up the UI so that it looks better. You see it in the screenshot, there's some stuff that's not even on the card. It's kind of not wrapping properly. It's not centered onto the card. It's kind of like, I don't even know, sitting to the side and just kind of messed up. Just fix that stuff, like aesthetically. And yeah, also clean up the add variable thing. It looks pretty shitty. I don't want it to be like a system notification. Just make it like a little text box, not a pop up system notification. Also, make sure that It can add multiple steps to a follow-up, so they can have:
1. One that sends immediately
2. One that sends an hour later
3. One that sends two days later with different templates
They can essentially just do it like a chain of different things, whether it's SMS or email. Then, also add an internal notification step to send to whatever their notification email is, or whatever email they decide to type in. They can either select one of the emails they have set for internal notifications, or they can just type in a new email/SMS as well.

Read changed files in the active git repository

- Fixed the Follow-up Automation page layout so everything stays centered and inside the cards (better max width + padding, `min-w-0` in the grid, and long “Available variables” text now wraps instead of blowing out the card): PortalFollowUpClient.tsx

  - Use booking notification emails (from Booking Automation notification emails / per-calendar overrides), or

- Pushed: `main` → `origin/main`; working tree is clean.

Key code touched:
- followUpAutomation.ts: settings v3 step-chains + scheduler uses per-step delays (up to years), back-compat upgrades.

User: Okay, that shit works pretty good. I mean, that shit already works. I already checked it out; it works. You can move on to Missed Call Text Back. You can move on to that one and just populate that tab with however you think it should work and look. But for this one, I know it should definitely be pretty minimalistic: a called list of missed calls, low automation of what you want to send whenever a call is missed, and then the list of the missed calls and the messages that were sent as a result of those. Completed: *Audit existing Twilio/call code* (1/5)

- Built out the Missed-Call Text Back tab (minimal settings + missed-calls list + activity feed) in page.tsx and PortalMissedCallTextBackClient.tsx.
- Added the portal settings API at route.ts.
- Added Twilio Voice webhooks:
  - Incoming call (returns TwiML `<Dial>`): [src/app/api/public/twilio/missed-call-textback/[token]/voice/route.ts](src/app/api/public/twilio/missed-call-textback/[token]/voice/route.ts)
  - Dial “action” callback (detect missed vs answered, send SMS reply): [src/app/api/public/twilio/missed-call-textback/[token]/dial-action/route.ts](src/app/api/public/twilio/missed-call-textback/[token]/dial-action/route.ts)
- Persistence is migration-free: settings + last ~200 events stored in `PortalServiceSetup.dataJson` via missedCallTextBack.ts.
- `npm run build` is green, and I committed + pushed to `origin/main`: commit `e8ed5e5` (“add Missed-Call Text Back portal tab with Twilio webhook + activity log”). Pushing `main` should trigger your Vercel deploy as usual.

If you want, I can also add a tiny “test webhook” button in the tab (simulates a missed call event without Twilio) so you can demo the UI even when Twilio isn’t configured.

User: All right, so as far as the environment variables, we're not putting manually in Vercel environment variables for every account's Twilio. That shit needs to be housed somewhere in there within the fucking portal. This isn't like this because each damn account, each different person, we might have a thousand people making accounts. They need to be able to put their own Twilio information in and do it that way, so it don't need to be an environment variable. But you get what I'm saying. Also make the on and off toggle a little more obvious because I can't tell if it says off—is it off? And whenever I hit on, because if I click off, does it turn on? It's confusing. Have a clear on and off beside each other. Don't make it go away in the button switch. Just make it obvious which one it is. And then you can commit, push, deploy these changes. Thanks.

- Twilio credentials are now stored per portal account (not Vercel env vars) and used for all portal SMS sends (missed-call replies, follow-up SMS, booking contact/reschedule).
- Missed Call Text Back now has a clearer explicit Off/On segmented control and a Twilio credentials editor directly in the tab.

**Key additions/changes**
- New per-owner Twilio integration + sender: portalTwilio.ts
- New portal API for Twilio creds (masked GET, save/clear PUT): route.ts
- Missed-call webhooks + settings now use per-owner Twilio config:  
  - [src/app/api/public/twilio/missed-call-textback/[token]/voice/route.ts](src/app/api/public/twilio/missed-call-textback/%5Btoken%5D/voice/route.ts)

  - route.ts
- Missed-call portal UI updates: PortalMissedCallTextBackClient.tsx

**Verification + deploy**
- `npm run build` passes.
- Committed and pushed to `origin/main` (commit `df76a0d`), which should trigger the deploy.

Appointment reminders are now set up in the Booking tab and deployed via cron.

- Added SMS appointment reminders settings + activity feed inside the Booking service UI: PortalBookingClient.tsx
- New portal API for reminders settings/events: route.ts
- New cron endpoint that sends reminders for owners who enabled it: route.ts
- Core logic (settings parsing, templating, dedupe, event log, sending via per-owner Twilio): appointmentReminders.ts
- Scheduled Vercel cron every 5 minutes: vercel.json

Notes:
- Reminder template supports `{name}` and `{when}`.
- Uses the per-account Twilio integration you just approved; if Twilio isn’t configured, reminders will show as “skipped” in activity.

Pushed to `origin/main` (commit `fa68316`) so Vercel should auto-deploy. If you want, I can add a second reminder (e.g. 24h + 1h) or email reminders too.

Ran terminal command:  git status --porcelain

Fixed — appointment reminders should now be visible and customizable, and they work step-based like follow-up automation.

- The main issue was discoverability: reminders were buried inside the Booking “Settings” grid and easy to miss. I added a dedicated top-level “Reminders” tab in the Booking service UI: PortalBookingClient.tsx
- Upgraded reminders settings to a multi-step model (v2) so users can add multiple reminders with different lead times + templates (default template included). Backwards compatible with the old single-reminder shape: appointmentReminders.ts
- The cron processor now sends reminders per enabled step and dedupes by `(bookingId, stepId)`.

Shipped
- `npm run build` passes.
- Committed + pushed to `origin/main` (commit `735707d`) so Vercel deploy should pick it up.

If you want it to mirror follow-up even closer, I can add “per-step enable toggle + delay units (minutes/hours/days)” UI polish, but the functionality is in place now.

- Added a dedicated portal page at page.tsx so `/portal/app/services/appointment-reminders` no longer hits the generic placeholder.
- Implemented the real setup UI in PortalAppointmentRemindersClient.tsx, reusing the existing reminders backend (`/api/portal/booking/reminders/settings`) with Off/On + multi-step editor + activity feed.

- Ran `npm run build` successfully and pushed to `main` (commit `a284ecf`).

- Lead-time input now supports **minutes / hours / days / weeks** (no more “2880 minutes” for 2 days). This is wired end-to-end: UI → API → cron processing.  
  - Engine + parsing/back-compat: appointmentReminders.ts  
  - UI (standalone tab): PortalAppointmentRemindersClient.tsx  
  - UI (Booking → Reminders tab): PortalBookingClient.tsx

- Appointment reminders can now be configured as **different sequences per calendar/booking link**:
  - Both UIs now have a **Calendar selector**, and saving uses `calendarId` scoping via query param.
  - API supports per-calendar settings: route.ts

- Bookings created via a calendar booking link now record `bookingId -> calendarId` so cron can apply the right sequence:
  - [src/app/api/public/booking/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/book/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/book/route.ts)

- Verified `npm run build` passes and pushed to `main` (commit `c8492ca`).

If you want, I can add a small “Default vs Override” hint (so it’s obvious when a calendar is inheriting defaults vs has its own custom steps).

Read changed files in the active git repository

Review Requests is now scaffolded end-to-end (portal setup + SMS sending + hosted page) and the old standalone Appointment Reminders service is hidden/redirected.

- Portal setup UI: PortalReviewsClient.tsx (multiple review links, delay in minutes/hours/days/weeks, SMS template, “Verified by Purely” toggle, recent activity, manual send by bookingId).
- Portal routes: settings/events/send at
  - route.ts
  - route.ts
  - route.ts
- Automated sending: cron endpoint + schedule
  - route.ts (protected by `REVIEWS_CRON_SECRET`)
  - vercel.json (runs every 5 minutes)
  - Engine lives in reviewRequests.ts (stores settings + sent dedupe keys + event log in `PortalServiceSetup.dataJson` under `serviceSlug="reviews"`).
- Hosted public pages:
  - Blog-style slug: [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx)
  - Booking-slug fallback: [src/app/book/[slug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/book/[slug]/reviews/page.tsx)
- Sidebar swap cleanup: Appointment Reminders removed from the catalog and the old page redirects to Booking:
  - catalog.ts
  - page.tsx

Read [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/.github)

- Pushed to `origin/main` (commit `ffd8090`), working tree clean.
- Reviews are now hosted on the public “website” surface at `/{siteSlug}/reviews` via [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx) (this `siteSlug` is the public blog/site slug, not the booking slug).
- The booking-based reviews route is deprecated and now hard-404s so reviews aren’t “under booking” anymore: [src/app/book/[slug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/book/[slug]/reviews/page.tsx).
- Review-request SMS now uses the hosted reviews page link when `publicPage.enabled` is on (otherwise it uses the direct destination URL): reviewRequests.ts.
- Manual send no longer requires typing a booking ID; it’s a searchable booking picker grid that sends from the row: PortalReviewsClient.tsx.
- “Send only if confirmed/happy”: the DB model only has booking statuses `SCHEDULED` and `CANCELED`, so I implemented the safe rule “only send after the appointment ends + not canceled”. If you want “happy/confirmed”, tell me what data should define that (e.g. internal outcome field, NPS, confirmation reply, etc.) and I’ll wire it in.

- Added the settings you’re asking for: Review Requests now has an explicit **Send mode** section with toggles for **Auto-send after appointments** and **Allow manual sends** in PortalReviewsClient.tsx.
- Wired those toggles into behavior:
  - Cron will **only** send when `automation.autoSend` is on.
  - Manual send will **only** work when `automation.manualSend` is on.
  - Both still enforce “only after the meeting is over” (appointment ended) in reviewRequests.ts.
- Ran `npm run build` successfully.
- Committed + pushed to `origin/main`: commit `6e798ac` (“Add review request automation mode settings”).

If you want “conditions” beyond that (e.g., only for specific calendars, only for certain appointment types), we’ll need a field on `PortalBooking` that indicates calendar/type—right now the booking model only has `SCHEDULED` vs `CANCELED`, so the only reliable trigger condition is “ended + not canceled + has phone.”

Calendar scoping for Review Requests is now finished end-to-end (UI + backend + DB), and it’s committed + pushed.

- Added selectable calendar scope in the Reviews settings UI (empty selection = all calendars), and manual-send now clearly disables “Send” when a booking’s calendar isn’t enabled (or the booking has no calendarId while a scope is set): PortalReviewsClient.tsx
- Persisted `calendarId` on bookings + added an index to support cron filtering: schema.prisma, migration.sql
- Booking creation via the calendar-specific public route now stores `calendarId`: [src/app/api/public/booking/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/book/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/u/%5BownerId%5D/%5BcalendarId%5D/book/route.ts)
- Cron + manual-send enforce the calendar allowlist via `automation.calendarIds`: reviewRequests.ts
- Fixed local builds by always running `prisma generate` during prebuild (migrations still skipped unless you opt in): prisma-prebuild.mjs

Notes:
- If you set “selected calendars” (non-empty list), any bookings created through slug-based booking URLs that don’t have a `calendarId` will be blocked from sending review requests (matches the backend safety rule).

Want me to also show the booking’s calendar on the “Recent activity” (events) list, so you can audit which calendar triggered each send?

**What’s done (and deployed)**
- Portal Reviews setup UX is now consistent and obvious (segmented `Off | On`, clear state), with a clickable preview link to the hosted reviews page: PortalReviewsClient.tsx
- “Verified by Purely” is treated as mandatory (no optional toggle/copy), and the public page styling is now readable + modern: [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx)
- Hosted reviews page is fully functional: star rating + text + file-photo uploads + shows recent reviews: [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/PublicReviewsClient.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/PublicReviewsClient.tsx)
- Portal inbox for reviews (list + archive/unarchive): route.ts, route.ts
- Booking load crash (“Unexpected end of JSON input”) mitigated by hardening the bookings API to always return JSON: route.ts
- Destinations are optional; the system works with the Purely-hosted page only. Outbound SMS now always uses the hosted `/{slug}/reviews` link as the primary link, and additionally appends any configured external destinations: reviewRequests.ts

**Data + API**
- New Prisma model to store submitted reviews + archive state: schema.prisma
- Migration added: migration.sql
- Public multipart submit endpoint (file uploads, supports many photos per submission with safety caps): [src/app/api/public/reviews/[siteSlug]/submit/route.ts](src/app/api/public/reviews/[siteSlug]/submit/route.ts)

**Verification**
- `npm run build` passes successfully (twice after the follow-up photo-limit change).
- `npm run lint` still fails, but due to lots of pre-existing repo lint violations (not specific to this feature set).

**Commits / Push**
- Changes are committed and pushed to `main` (2 commits): functional reviews system + follow-up to allow many photos per review submission.

If you want, I can start a local dev server (`npm run dev`) and give you the exact URLs/flows to smoke-test (portal setup → preview → submit review → inbox archive).

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && set -a && source .env && set +a && cat <<'SQL' | npx prisma db execute --stdin --url "$DIRECT_URL"
DO $$
BEGIN
  IF EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM information_schema.columns
    WHERE table_schema = 'public'
      AND table_name = 'PortalBooking'
      AND column_name = 'calendarId'
  ) THEN
    RAISE NOTICE 'OK: PortalBooking.calendarId exists';
  ELSE
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'MISSING: PortalBooking.calendarId';
  END IF;
END $$;
SQL

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && set -a && source .env && set +a && cat <<'SQL' | npx prisma db execute --stdin --url "$DIRECT_URL"
DO $$
DECLARE
  regclass_name text;
BEGIN
  SELECT to_regclass('public."PortalReview"')::text INTO regclass_name;
  IF regclass_name IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'MISSING: PortalReview table';
  ELSE
    RAISE NOTICE 'OK: PortalReview table exists (%).', regclass_name;
  END IF;
END $$;
SQL

Ran terminal command:  set -a && source .env && set +a && cat prisma/migrations/20260205133000_client_reviews/migration.sql | npx prisma db execute --stdin --url "$DIRECT_URL"

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && set -a && source .env && set +a && cat <<'SQL' | npx prisma db execute --stdin --url "$DIRECT_URL"
-- Portal reviews (hosted reviews page submissions)

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "PortalReview" (
  "id" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "ownerId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "rating" INTEGER NOT NULL,
  "name" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "body" TEXT,
  "email" TEXT,
  "phone" TEXT,
  "photoUrls" JSONB,
  "archivedAt" TIMESTAMP(3),
  "createdAt" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  "updatedAt" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

  CONSTRAINT "PortalReview_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

DO $$
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM pg_constraint
    WHERE conname = 'PortalReview_ownerId_fkey'
  ) THEN
    ALTER TABLE "PortalReview"
      ADD CONSTRAINT "PortalReview_ownerId_fkey"
      FOREIGN KEY ("ownerId") REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;
  END IF;
END $$;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS "PortalReview_ownerId_archivedAt_createdAt_idx"
  ON "PortalReview"("ownerId", "archivedAt", "createdAt");
SQL

Ran terminal command:  cd /Users/jaylan/purely-automation && set -a && source .env && set +a && cat <<'SQL' | npx prisma db execute --stdin --url "$DIRECT_URL"
DO $$
DECLARE
  regclass_name text;
BEGIN
  SELECT to_regclass('public."PortalReview"')::text INTO regclass_name;
  IF regclass_name IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'MISSING: PortalReview table';
  ELSE
    RAISE NOTICE 'OK: PortalReview table exists (%).', regclass_name;
  END IF;
END $$;
SQL

Updated the public Reviews page styling to match the Purely blogs/home vibe, and pushed it so Vercel can deploy it.

- Restyled the whole layout (header, hero, spacing, cards) to match the client blogs template look: [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx)
- Replaced the “dot + Verified by Purely” thing with a clean verified badge that uses a real icon (checkmark) and keeps it simple: [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx)
- Added the “more businesses on purely” horizontal scroller at the bottom (auto-populates from businesses with public reviews enabled)
- Added a footer-style CTA block similar to the blogs pages (“want a page like this?” + buttons)
- Updated the rating buttons so they use the business brand color (no more black boxes): [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/PublicReviewsClient.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/PublicReviewsClient.tsx)
- Build is green and it’s pushed (commit `3653838`).

Done. Two fixes shipped: brand marks + review submit 500.

- Brand/logo fixes on the public reviews page:
  - Replaced the placeholder dot with the Purely play mark when there’s no business logo.
  - Purely link in the header now uses your brand logo from the brand folder (not a dot).
  - Verified badge now uses the Purely play mark instead of the dot/check text combo.
  - Updated in: [src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx](src/app/[siteSlug]/reviews/page.tsx)

- Fixed the HTTP 500 when submitting reviews (especially with photos):
  - Root cause was writing uploads to `/public/...` (Vercel can’t write to disk at runtime).
  - Photos are now stored in Supabase Postgres in a new table and served back via an API URL.
  - Updated endpoint: [src/app/api/public/reviews/[siteSlug]/submit/route.ts](src/app/api/public/reviews/[siteSlug]/submit/route.ts)
  - New photo-serving endpoint: [src/app/api/public/reviews/photos/[photoId]/route.ts](src/app/api/public/reviews/photos/[photoId]/route.ts)
  - New DB table + migration: migration.sql and schema update in schema.prisma

- Live DB update: I created the `PortalReviewPhoto` table in Supabase already.
- Deployed: pushed commit `777c85d` to `main` so Vercel will deploy it.

Now refresh `https://purelyautomation.com/home2smart/reviews` and try submitting again (with a photo). It should submit successfully and the uploaded image should render in “Recent reviews” right away.

Searched for files matching `**/.github/copilot-instructions.md`, 1 match

**What’s now implemented**
- Portal-configurable public reviews form: toggle Email/Phone + required flags, and add/edit/reorder/remove custom questions (incl. single/multi choice options) in PortalReviewsClient.tsx.
- Server-side enforcement + persistence: the submit API now validates required fields/questions from settings, stores `answersJson`, and respects toggles in [src/app/api/public/reviews/[siteSlug]/submit/route.ts](src/app/api/public/reviews/[siteSlug]/submit/route.ts).
- “2 out of 3” identity merge: bookings + reviews now best-effort create/merge a `PortalContact` and write `contactId` on both sides (drift-hardened if the table/columns aren’t deployed yet) via portalContacts.ts and booking routes:
  - [src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/book/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/[slug]/book/route.ts)
  - [src/app/api/public/booking/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/book/route.ts](src/app/api/public/booking/u/[ownerId]/[calendarId]/book/route.ts)

**DB migration**
- Added the idempotent migration to create `PortalContact` and add `contactId`/`answersJson`: migration.sql
- To activate contact-linking in prod, make sure migrations run (your build output shows deploy is skipped unless enabled). Either run `RUN_PRISMA_MIGRATIONS=1 npx prisma migrate deploy` in the deploy environment, or apply the SQL in Supabase.

- Cleaned up the “Custom questions” editor layout so controls wrap cleanly and don’t overlap (moved the question row to a grid with a fixed right column, tightened button sizing, and improved wrapping) in PortalReviewsClient.tsx.

GitHub Copilot: I’ll pull the exact migration SQL we generated (idempotent) so you can run it directly in Supabase, matching your Prisma schema/table names.

Read [](file:///Users/jaylan/purely-automation/prisma/migrations/20260206211500_review_replies_qa/migration.sql)

Searched for regex `model PortalReview\b|model PortalReviewQuestion\b|@@map\(|@map\(` (`**/prisma/schema.prisma`), 2 results

Run this SQL in Supabase (SQL Editor). It’s safe to run multiple times (uses `IF NOT EXISTS` / constraint guard):

```sql
-- Business replies on reviews + public Q&A.

-- Add business reply fields to PortalReview.
ALTER TABLE "PortalReview" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "businessReply" TEXT;
ALTER TABLE "PortalReview" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "businessReplyAt" TIMESTAMP(3);

-- Create PortalReviewQuestion table (idempotent).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "PortalReviewQuestion" (
  "id" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "ownerId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "name" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "question" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "answer" TEXT,
  "answeredAt" TIMESTAMP(3),
  "createdAt" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  "updatedAt" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  CONSTRAINT "PortalReviewQuestion_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

DO $$
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM pg_constraint
    WHERE conname = 'PortalReviewQuestion_ownerId_fkey'
  ) THEN
    ALTER TABLE "PortalReviewQuestion"
      ADD CONSTRAINT "PortalReviewQuestion_ownerId_fkey"
      FOREIGN KEY ("ownerId") REFERENCES "User"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;
  END IF;
END $$;

-- Indexes (idempotent).
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS "PortalReviewQuestion_ownerId_createdAt_idx" ON "PortalReviewQuestion"("ownerId", "createdAt");
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS "PortalReviewQuestion_ownerId_answeredAt_idx" ON "PortalReviewQuestion"("ownerId", "answeredAt");
```

After you run it, the “replies not enabled” message should go away immediately (at worst, after a redeploy/restart if the environment caches schema checks). Want me to also give you a quick “verification query” you can run to confirm the columns/table exist?
